title: Claude Code Session File Schema Documentation
description: This document describes the structure of session log files created by Claude Code (Anthropic's CLI/IDE extension). These files contain actual Anthropic API token counts and can be used by the Copilot Token Tracker extension to report on Claude Code token usage.

changes: Initial schema documentation based on observed session files from Claude Code v2.1.86

officialSourceReferences.description: Claude Code is Anthropic's official CLI for Claude. Session files are written locally and never synced by default.
officialSourceReferences.note: The session file schema is internal to Claude Code and may evolve across versions. The fields documented here were observed in Claude Code v2.1.5 and v2.1.86.

keyDifferencesFromCopilot.description: Notable differences between Claude Code session files and GitHub Copilot session files
keyDifferencesFromCopilot.actualTokenCounts: Claude Code stores ACTUAL token counts from the Anthropic API in every assistant message. No estimation needed.
keyDifferencesFromCopilot.cacheTokens: Includes separate fields for cache_creation_input_tokens and cache_read_input_tokens (prompt caching).
keyDifferencesFromCopilot.noContentText: User message content is stored but assistant content is also stored in full — however, for token counting purposes only the usage object is needed.
keyDifferencesFromCopilot.messageLinkedList: Messages form a linked list via parentUuid, enabling conversation tree reconstruction.
keyDifferencesFromCopilot.subagents: Sub-agent conversations (spawned via the Agent tool) are stored in separate JSONL files under a subagents/ subdirectory.
keyDifferencesFromCopilot.statsCache: A separate stats-cache.json file at ~/.claude/ contains pre-aggregated daily usage stats maintained by Claude Code itself.

sessions.description: Main session JSONL file. One file per Claude Code session (conversation). Contains all user/assistant messages with actual API token usage.

subagents.description: Sub-agent session files. Created when the main session spawns a sub-agent via the Agent tool. Same JSONL format as main sessions but with agentId field and isSidechain: true on all entries.

statsCache.description: Pre-aggregated daily usage statistics maintained by Claude Code. Updated periodically (not necessarily after every session). May be stale if Claude Code has not been run recently.

activeSessions.description: Per-process session pointer file written while Claude Code is running. Contains the PID, session UUID, working directory, start time, kind (interactive/non-interactive), and entrypoint.

projectHashFormat.description: The {project-hash} segment in the path is derived from the project's working directory
projectHashFormat.algorithm: The absolute path is lowercased, drive letter colon is removed, and all path separators (backslashes on Windows, forward slashes on Unix) are replaced with dashes.

fileFormat.description: JSON Lines format — one complete JSON object per line. Each line is an independent event/message.

eventTypes.description: Each line in the JSONL file is one of the following event types, distinguished by the 'type' field

commonFields.description: Fields present on most or all event types

type.description: Event type discriminator. One of: user, assistant, queue-operation, file-history-snapshot, ai-title
type.usedBy: Required — used to determine how to parse the rest of the line

timestamp.usedBy: Used to determine interaction timestamps and session duration

sessionId.description: The session UUID this event belongs to. Matches the filename.
sessionId.usedBy: Used to correlate events; filename is sufficient

userEventSchema.description: A user message turn. Contains the user's input to Claude.

uuid.description: Unique identifier for this message. Referenced by the assistant's parentUuid.
uuid.usedBy: Can be used to reconstruct conversation tree

parentUuid.description: UUID of the preceding message this replies to. null for the first message in a session.

promptId.description: Groups a user message with all associated assistant messages (including streaming fragments). Shared across multiple assistant events for the same turn.

isSidechain.description: true for sub-agent sessions, false for the main conversation thread
isSidechain.usedBy: Can be used to separate main vs sub-agent token usage

message.description: The actual message content in Anthropic API format

content.description: Message content. Array of content blocks for structured content, or a plain string for simple messages (seen in subagent warmup messages).

tool_result_block.description: Result returned from a tool call, fed back to the model

tool_use_id.description: Matches the tool_use id in the preceding assistant message

permissionMode.description: The permission mode active for this message. e.g., 'default', 'acceptEdits'
permissionMode.usedBy: Not currently used for token counting

userType.usedBy: Not currently used for token counting

entrypoint.description: How Claude Code was launched. e.g., 'claude-vscode' (VSCode extension), 'claude-cli' (terminal)
entrypoint.usedBy: Can be used to identify the editor/entrypoint

cwd.description: Absolute path of the working directory when the message was sent

version.description: Claude Code CLI version that created this message
version.usedBy: Not currently used for token counting

gitBranch.description: Git branch active in the working directory at the time of the message
gitBranch.usedBy: Not currently used for token counting

toolUseResult.description: Present when this user message is the result of a tool execution (i.e., the model invoked a tool and this message contains its output back to the model). Contains tool result metadata.
toolUseResult.usedBy: Not currently used for token counting

sourceToolAssistantUUID.description: UUID of the assistant message that triggered this tool result. Present only on tool result user messages.

content.text: analyze the use of the extensions in this repo

assistantEventSchema.description: An assistant response turn. Contains the model's output and ACTUAL token usage from the Anthropic API. A single user turn may produce multiple assistant events (streaming fragments, tool calls).
assistantEventSchema.note: Multiple assistant events may share the same requestId (streaming fragments from one API call). Only the final event per requestId (with stop_reason != null) has complete token usage.

uuid.description: Unique identifier for this assistant message event
uuid.usedBy: Referenced by the next user message's parentUuid

parentUuid.description: UUID of the user message this is a response to

requestId.description: Anthropic API request ID (req_...). Shared by streaming fragments of the same API call.
requestId.usedBy: De-duplication: only count tokens once per unique requestId

isSidechain.usedBy: Can separate main vs sub-agent token usage

slug.description: Short human-readable identifier for the message

message.description: The Anthropic API message response object

model.description: The Claude model that generated this response
model.usedBy: Used to identify the model for per-model token breakdowns

thinking_block.description: Extended thinking content (internal reasoning, shown separately in UI)

signature.description: Cryptographic signature verifying this thinking block came from the model

tool_use_block.description: A tool call made by the assistant

stop_reason.description: Why the model stopped generating. null for streaming fragments, 'end_turn' for complete responses, 'tool_use' when a tool was called, 'max_tokens' if truncated.
stop_reason.usedBy: De-duplication: only count usage from events where stop_reason != null

stop_sequence.description: The stop sequence that caused the model to stop, if applicable

usage.description: ACTUAL token counts from the Anthropic API. Unlike Copilot session files which require estimation, these are exact counts.
usage.usedBy: Primary source for token counting — no estimation needed

input_tokens.description: New input tokens processed that were NOT served from cache. Small when prompt caching is effective.
input_tokens.usedBy: Add to total input token count

output_tokens.description: Output tokens generated by the model
output_tokens.usedBy: Add to total output token count

cache_creation_input_tokens.description: Tokens written to the prompt cache in this request. These are charged at a higher rate than regular input tokens.
cache_creation_input_tokens.usedBy: Track separately for accurate cost calculation; add to total input for usage volume

cache_read_input_tokens.description: Tokens read from the prompt cache (not re-processed). Charged at a much lower rate. Indicates effective context reuse.
cache_read_input_tokens.usedBy: Track separately; significant indicator of caching efficiency

cache_creation.description: Breakdown of cache creation by TTL tier (ephemeral prompt caching)
cache_creation.usedBy: Detailed cache analysis; not required for basic token counting

web_search_requests.description: Number of web search requests made by the model

web_fetch_requests.description: Number of web fetch (URL read) requests made

server_tool_use.usedBy: Track tool usage; relevant if server-side tools are billed separately

service_tier.description: API service tier used for this request

inference_geo.description: Geographic region where inference was performed

iterations.description: Per-iteration token counts for multi-step reasoning (when applicable)
iterations.usedBy: Not used for token counting (totals already in input/output_tokens)

entrypoint.description: Same as user event — how Claude Code was launched

cwd.description: Working directory when the response was generated

queueOperationEventSchema.description: Internal queue management event, emitted at the start and end of each message exchange. Used by Claude Code for internal state management.
queueOperationEventSchema.usedBy: Not used for token counting — skip these events

operation.description: Whether the operation is queuing a request (enqueue) or processing it (dequeue)

fileHistorySnapshotEventSchema.description: File state snapshot recorded at each user message turn to support undo functionality. Captures the state of any tracked files before Claude makes changes.
fileHistorySnapshotEventSchema.usedBy: Not used for token counting — skip these events

messageId.description: The user message UUID this snapshot is associated with

trackedFileBackups.description: Map of file paths to their backed-up content. Empty object if no files were modified yet.

isSnapshotUpdate.description: Whether this is an update to an existing snapshot rather than a new snapshot

aiTitleEventSchema.description: AI-generated session title, written once per session after the first exchange. Used as a human-readable label for the session in history views.
aiTitleEventSchema.usedBy: Can be used as a display name for the session

aiTitle.description: Human-readable title summarizing the session content
aiTitle.example: Analyze repository extensions and local file storage

example.aiTitle: Analyze repository extensions and local file storage

subagentFiles.description: When Claude Code spawns a sub-agent via the Agent tool, a separate JSONL file is created under {session-uuid}/subagents/{agent-id}.jsonl. The format is identical to the main session JSONL with the following differences:

agentId.description: Short hex identifier for the sub-agent (e.g., 'a0d1d2d'). Present on all user and assistant events.

messageFormat.note: Subagent user messages may use a plain string for message.content instead of an array of content blocks (e.g., warmup messages)

statsCacheSchema.description: Schema for ~/.claude/stats-cache.json — a pre-aggregated usage summary maintained by Claude Code itself.
statsCacheSchema.note: This file may be stale (lastComputedDate may be days or weeks behind). It only covers sessions that were present when the cache was last computed. For accurate up-to-date stats, parse the individual session JSONL files directly.

lastComputedDate.description: Date when the cache was last recomputed by Claude Code

tokensByModel.description: Map of model name to total tokens (sum of input + output)

modelUsage.description: Lifetime token usage aggregated per model

costUSD.description: Calculated cost in USD (may be 0 if not computed)

hourCounts.description: Map of hour (0-23) to number of sessions started in that hour

extensionIntegrationNotes.description: Guidance for integrating Claude Code session parsing into the Copilot Token Tracker extension

tokenCounting.approach: Use the 'usage' object in assistant events directly — no estimation needed
tokenCounting.deduplication: Multiple assistant events can share a requestId (streaming). Only count usage from the event with stop_reason != null, or sum unique requestIds.
tokenCounting.inputTokens: message.usage.input_tokens + message.usage.cache_creation_input_tokens + message.usage.cache_read_input_tokens = effective total input tokens processed
tokenCounting.thinkingTokens: Thinking tokens are included in output_tokens — no separate field
tokenCounting.interactionCount: Count distinct promptIds (or count user events with isSidechain=false)

sessionDiscovery.excludeSubagents: Exclude files under */subagents/* for main-session-only stats, or include for complete totals
sessionDiscovery.projectMapping: Reverse the project-hash algorithm to recover the original cwd: replace leading letter dash with letter colon (Windows), replace remaining dashes with appropriate separators

modelIdentification.note: Unlike Copilot where model is embedded in a freeform string, Claude Code provides the exact API model ID

modeDetection.agentMode: Check if any assistant event in the session has a tool_use content block with name='Agent'
modeDetection.toolCallDetection: Count tool_use content blocks across all assistant events
modeDetection.toolNames: Available from tool_use blocks: Bash, Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Agent, WebSearch, WebFetch, etc.

timestamp.firstInteraction: timestamp of first user event with isSidechain=false
timestamp.lastInteraction: timestamp of last assistant event with isSidechain=false and stop_reason != null

notes.actualVsEstimated: The most significant difference from Copilot: Claude Code records ACTUAL Anthropic API token counts. The input_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens + cache_read_input_tokens fields together give the true context size.
notes.promptCachingImpact: In typical Claude Code sessions, cache_read_input_tokens dominates because the system prompt and conversation history are cached. Only new content generates input_tokens. For cost calculation, the three fields must be weighted differently.
notes.multipleAssistantEvents: A single user turn may generate many assistant events as the model streams content or makes multiple tool calls. Each has its own usage accounting. Sum by unique requestId or use stop_reason to find final counts.
notes.subagentTokens: Sub-agents run as separate API calls and have their own token usage. Their JSONL files are separate, so including or excluding subagents is a clean decision.
notes.noContentEstimation: Unlike Copilot Token Tracker's character-based estimation, Claude Code session files make estimation unnecessary — always use the usage.input_tokens / output_tokens fields directly.
notes.versionObserved: Schema documented from Claude Code v2.1.5 and v2.1.86 (March 2026). Fields may be added in future versions.
